steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $\frac{x+7}{4} = \frac{x+7}{16}$ has the same numerator on both sides but different denominators. The only way two fractions with equal numerators and unequal denominators can be equal is if the numerator is $0$. So $x + 7 = 0$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $3$ and $7$: does not contain 0. ✗
  • Choice B — $-3$ and $3$: contains 0. ✓
  • Choice C — $-8$ and $-5$: does not contain 0. ✗
  • Choice D — $8$ and $13$: does not contain 0. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
